question: Is the colorful poster content from Image 2 (jumping woman, Chinese text, geometric shapes) clearly visible inside the lightbox of Image 1?
Are the extraneous background elements from Image 2 (wooden table, books, coffee cup, hands) completely excluded from the final image?
Does the inserted poster's perspective correctly align with the angled plane of the lightbox frame?
Are the original staples or thumbtacks from Image 1 preserved in their exact locations, appearing to pin down the new poster?
Is the specific physical damage (tear and curl) at the lower-left corner preserved, with the new poster content conforming to the shape of the original tear?
Does the torn corner at the lower-left reveal the edge of the underlying layer (as seen in Image 1) rather than a void or the background from Image 2?
Are strips of tape applied to both the top and right edges of the poster?
Is the applied tape semi-transparent, allowing the underlying poster graphics to show through, and visually consistent with the texture in Image 3?
Does the color temperature of the inserted poster match the cool, overcast ambient lighting of the bus stop scene in Image 1?
Are the original glass reflections and surface dirt from Image 1 visible on top of the new poster content?
Do the highlights on the added tape strips align with the diffuse, cool lighting direction of the bus stop scene?
Is the bus stop structure (metal frame, glass panels) and the street background preserved without distortion or unintended changes?
Is the Chinese text on the poster ('全新能量! 释放自我!') legible and free from distortion?
Is the final image free from visible blending artifacts like white edges, halos, or harsh seam lines around the poster and tape?
